clinical_criteria: At least five attacks for migraine without aura diagnosis
At least two attacks for migraine with aura diagnosis
Headache attacks lasting 4 to 72 hours (untreated or unsuccessfully treated; 2-72 hours in children under 18)
At least two headache characteristics: unilateral location, pulsating quality, moderate or severe pain intensity, aggravation by routine physical activity
At least one accompanying symptom: nausea/vomiting, or photophobia and phonophobia
≥4 codes in distinct encounters for migraine without aura or unclassified
≥1 codes in distinct encounters for migraine with aura
≥2 codes in distinct encounters for unclassified migraine alone
≥2 codes in distinct encounters for hemiplegic migraine
Motor symptoms in hemiplegic migraine last <72 hours
At least one aura symptom spreads gradually over ≥5 minutes
Aura accompanied or followed within 60 minutes by headache
Evidence of reversible motor weakness for hemiplegic migraine
algorithm_summary: The migraine phenotype algorithm classifies patients into four main categories: hemiplegic migraine, migraine with aura, migraine without aura, and unclassified migraine. It uses specific ICD-9 and ICD-10 codes with frequency thresholds (≥4 encounters for without aura, ≥1 for with aura, ≥2 for unclassified and hemiplegic). The algorithm excludes patients with other brain disorders and tumors but explicitly does not exclude epilepsy. Cases are identified by relevant migraine ICD codes, while controls have no nervous system disease codes. Manual chart review is required for hemiplegic migraine to confirm reversible motor weakness. Optional text mining can identify associated symptoms to support classification.
